passage.title: Lab Table Label Project
passage.text: The science room team wanted first graders to understand experiment steps more easily. At first, the group used one chart packed with tiny labels and long arrows, but students could not tell which science step came first. A first-grade test class said "I can read some of it, but I do not know the first step." After hearing that, the team split the science task into one step per card and placed sample tools under the matching cards. They also set a mini reminder sign beside each table so students could compare the real tool with the picture right away. On the next test day, most students finished the test steps with fewer teacher reminders. The team noticed that one clear step at a time worked better than dense directions. Because of that result, the team decided to update the rest of the lab tables in the same style.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
Improving science directions so younger students can follow experiments
Painting a room with brighter colors
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: Students could not tell what to do next
Students could not tell which science step came first
The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the team place under the matching cards?
choices: Sample tools
Larger arrows
Short signs
Color blocks
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does dense mean in the seventh sentence?
choices: A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
Packed too closely together
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
The redesign showed one science step more clearly at a time
It made the room much larger
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Ask students to memorize everything
Update more science tables with the same clearer system
Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
